client_secret.description: This is the client_secret provided to the developer for accessing the api. Will match with the client_id

username.description: This is the username and logic for the user. This may look like a uid (user@domain) or could be something else if setup diffferent including a email address potentially.

password.description: This is the password for the user requesting access.

vendor.description: The SSO provider/vendor being used for authentication.

idtoken.description: Will likely be subscriber as the value here to defierentiate between authenticator for api/portal and mfa with admin ui

scope.description: This will be the access_token or jwt that was given with the scope of mfa_required

grant_type.description: This is the type of authentication request being made. 'mfa' would be needed in the second request with MFA passcode.

call-id.description: This will be the callid for the requested call. This should be a random string generated by your application and stored if you want to read back the call later or end it.

dial-rule-application.description: The application can be used to help with next destination selection.

call-term-user.description: This will be the destination or termination number for the call request. This will be used once the call is connected to the orgination number.

call-orig-user.description: This field will allow you to select the device, user or number the first leg of the call request will connect to. If left blank the origination will be the user@domain of the user making the request.

auto-answer-enabled.description: If enabled it will request auto answer headers for the first leg (origination) of the call.

caller-id-number.description: This setting will allow for the callerid of the termination leg to be set, by default it will be the preconfigured callerid of the user making the request.

callback-caller-id-number.description: This is the callerid that will be used when calling the first leg(callback) of the call request. If left empty it will default to the user's caller id.

call-term-added-sip-header.description: This field allows for a added sip header on the destination leg of the call.

dial-rule-dial-plan.description: This is the dial plan name the dial rule exists in.

dial-rule-matching-to-uri.description: This is the main digit string match for the dial translation.

dialrule.description: This is the dialrule id that will be given with each read action that will be needed to read back individual value, update specific rules or delete rules. If wanting to programatically generate the dial rule id is a base64 encoding of the concatination with "||" of the following fields in order, dail-rule-matching-to-uri, dail-rule-matching-from-uri, dail-rule-matching-day-of-week, dail-rule-matching-start-date, dail-rule-matching-end-date, dail-rule-matching-start-time, dail-rule-matching-end-time

enabled.description: When enabled  this setting will turn on voicemail for the user.

dial-rule-matching-from-uri.description: This is a source match that would allow for a specific dial rule match based on the caller as well as the forward destination of dial-rule-matching-to-uri.

dial-rule-matching-day-of-week.description: This is a field that will control the DOW (day of week) match for this rule. Default is * or to match all the time, but valid format will allow any single day of the week 0-6 (sunday-saturday) or a range in square brackets like [0-6]

dial-rule-matching-start-date.description: This is the start of a the valid date range for this rule.  Keep to * if you do not want to use date range for this rule.

dial-rule-matching-end-date.description: This is the end of a the valid date range for this rule.  Keep to * if you do not want to use date range for this rule.

dial-rule-matching-start-time.description: This is the start of a the valid time range for this rule.  Keep to * if you do not want to use time range for this rule. The format is a valid HH:MM format from 00:00 to 23:59. The timezone would be the user's timezone if passing through a user already.

dial-rule-matching-end-time.description: This is the end of a the valid time range for this rule.  Keep to * if you do not want to use time range for this rule. The format is a valid HH:MM format from 00:00 to 23:59. The timezone would be the user's timezone if passing through a user already.

dial-rule-parameter.description: This is the application parameter field that can be used for additional settings based on the dial-rule-application. Default is empty string.

dial-rule-translation-destination-scheme.description: This setting controls the "sip schema" on the destination, in most all case setting to "[*]" is correct.

dial-rule-translation-destination-user.description: This is the forward destination the call will be sent to in partnership with the application (dial-rule-application) and host (dial-rule-translation-destination-host). This might be a user, queue or offnet number depending on the application.

dial-rule-translation-destination-host.description: This is the forward destination host the call will be sent to in partnership with the application (dial-rule-application) and user (dial-rule-translation-destination-user). This might be the domain name or a connection name if trying to route offnet.

dial-rule-translation-source-name.description: This field will allow customization of the source "name" often used for the caller id name or the the descriptive quoted value outside of the sip uri. Defaults to [*] but you can add a prefix for example with something like this, sales_[*]

dial-rule-translation-source-scheme.description: This setting controls the "sip schema" on the destination, in most all case setting to "[*]" is correct.

dial-rule-translation-source-user.description: This field will allow customization of the source "user" which is often the caller id number or the value before the @ in then SIP uri. Defaults to [*] but you can modify and include [*] as the previous value. An example would be using a token like <OwnCidNmbr> to replace with a user's caller id number.

dial-rule-translation-source-host.description: This field will allow customization of the source "host" which is  the value after the @ in then SIP uri. Defaults to [*] but you can modify and include [*] as the previous value. An example would be using a token like <OwnDomain> to replace with a user's domain

dial-rule-description.description: This is a description of the number/rule that can be used for defintion or tracking purposes.

phonenumber.description: This is the main phonenumber setting. Should be a  formated in a e164 format with country count and number. US domestic numbers should be similiar to 18005551234

enabled.description: When enabled  this setting will turn on voicemail for the user.

dial-rule-parameter.description: This is the application parameter field that can be used for additional settings based on the dial-rule-application. Default is empty string.

dial-rule-translation-destination-user.description: This is the forward destination the call will be sent to in partnership with the application (dial-rule-application) and host (dial-rule-translation-destination-host). This might be a user, queue or offnet number depending on the application.

dial-rule-translation-destination-host.description: This is the forward destination host the call will be sent to in partnership with the application (dial-rule-application) and user (dial-rule-translation-destination-user). This might be the domain name or a connection name if trying to route offnet.

dial-rule-translation-source-name.description: This field will allow customization of the source "name" often used for the caller id name or the the descriptive quoted value outside of the sip uri. Defaults to [*] but you can add a prefix for example with something like this, sales_[*]

dial-rule-description.description: This is a description of the number/rule that can be used for defintion or tracking purposes.

unique-id.description: This is a unique id that will be generated automatically on new contact create.

time-frame.description: This is the main timeframe name that will be used as the name and will be used in conjuction with answer rules.

time-range-data.description: This is an array of time range objects that will make up the timeframe.

ordinal-order.description: This will be a unique index and orger for the specific timerange inside of the given timeframe.

start-date.description: This is field will be set for the start of a calender based time range in the format of YYYY-MM-DD. To disable the start of a range use "now".

end-date.description: This is field will be set for the end of a calender based time range in the format of YYYY-MM-DD. To disable the start of a range use "never".

anyOf.description: Integer can be used 0-6 for day in week.
String of * can be used for all days.

day-of-week-number.description: This is field will be set for a specific day of the week ( 0-6 / Sunday - Saturday) for this given time range. * would be used for all days of the week.

start-time.description: This is the time in HH:MM format for the given time range. This will be the start of the window and will be based on the user's time-zone.

end-time.description: This is the time in HH:MM format for the given time range. This will be the end of the window and will be based on the user's time-zone.

invert-match.description: This setting when enable will request caller id blocking on outbound calls requesting privacy settings.  This may lower the ability to connect certain calls if not setup properly to support privacy settings.

emergency-address-id.description: This is an autogenerated ID for the Address.

address-name.description: This is the name label for the address

caller-name.description: This is the caller's name for the address

address-line-1.description: This is the address line 1 for the address

address-line-2.description: This is the address line 2 for the address

address-city.description: This is the city for the address

address-state-province-abbreviation.description: This is the state or province (Canadian) abbreviation for the address

address-postal-code.description: This is the postal code for the address

address-country-abbreviation.description: This is the country appreviation for the address

address-location-description.description: This is an optional location description for the address

ip-address-public.description: This is the public IP of the client window creating the address.

address-formatted-pidflo.description: This is a return value of the formatted PIDFLO standardized value for the address

carrier.description: This is the carrier name for the address

count-users-configured.description: This is the count of how many users are using the particular address

address-formatted-pretty.description: This is a human readable format of the address

address-is-default-for-site.description: This is whether the address is a default for the site

address-callback-number.description: This will match the callback number

address-name.description: This is the name label for the address

caller-name.description: This is the caller's name for the address

address-line-1.description: This is the address line 1 for the address

address-line-2.description: This is the address line 2 for the address

address-country-abbreviation.description: This is the country appreviation for the address

address-location-description.description: This is an optional location description for the address

ip-address-public.description: This is the public IP of the client window creating the address.

If address-formatted-pidflo is used, fill in address-line-1, address-city, etc for address information

carrier.description: This is the carrier name for the address

count-users-configured.description: This is the count of how many users are using the particular address

address-formatted-pretty.description: This is a human readable format of the address

address-is-default-for-site.description: This is whether the address is a default for the site

address-formatted-pidflo.description: Standardized values and fields of the address. May be different depending on carrier

reseller.description: The name of the Reseller. This is the main ID for the group which will be used to link domains for ownership.

description.description: This description can be used to give a more complete description and notes for this reseller.

domains-total.description: This is the total number of domains in this Reseller's account.

users-total.description: This is the total number of users in all domains owned by this reseller.

email-send-smtp-hostname.description: This is the SMTP server hostname

email-send-smtp-port.description: This is the SMTP port for sending emails.

email-send-smtp-username.description: This is the username used in connecting to SMTP  server for sending email.

email-send-smtp-password.description: This is the password used in connecting to SMTP  server for sending email.

active-calls-against-license-count.description: This is the current active calls counting against the license including on net calls.

active-calls-offnet-current.description: This is the current active calls across all domains that are calls coming from or going to offnet numbers.

examples: Used by Vendor Xyz for Billing Automation
description.description: This is the main description/name for the API key that should describe its purpose for tracking and understanding its intended use.

key-id.description: This is the Key ID that will identify the key and allow for managment, audit tracking and monitoring.

key.description: This is the key that will need to be saved securly for use in accessing the API. It will only be shown once.

reseller.description: This is the reseller that will limit the API key to access the resellers data set.

user-scope.description: This is the scope that will be be used to enforce permissions for this API key and access.

ip-address.description: Optional IP restrictions for API to require access from matching IP or subnets. Multipe ips/subnets can be provided comma seperated

readonly.description: If enabled it will limit any get Scope to a read only verion providing only GET acces to the read actions. POST, PUT and DELETE will be rejected.

can-create-keys.description: If enabled it will allow this key to make keys for lesser scopes then itself. It will not allow creations of scopes same or higher access level.

anyOf.description: A string with a specific model or "*" for all.

items.description: A string with a specific model or "*" for all. The specific models reference the API's scope defintions stored in the DB.

anyOf.description: An array of models.

allowed-models.description: This is an optional limitation to the ability of this apikey. The default is  * or all models, but can be set to a specific model or array of models allowing specific access. The "models" are linked to objects as defined in the scope definition and might have varies terminolofy from the api itself.

created-datetime.description: The datetime that the key was created.

lastused-datetime.description: The datetime of the last time the apikey was used for API access. The timestamp is acurrate up to the hour to avoid overhead in tracking high volume requests

filename.description: This is the filename without and path or folder. Should end in .wav

ordinal-order.description: This is the index of the audio file.

file-duration-seconds.description: This value is the length of the file in seconds.

file-size-kilobytes.description: This value will be a formated filesize of the file in kilobytes

created-datetime.description: This is the timestamp of create time fo the file

file-access-url.description: This will be URL of the file for remote playback.

file-script-text.description: This is the script that was used for text to speech and also used to create a new file.

file-source.description: This is the source of the file, it will show how the file was created originally.

text-to-speech-voice-id.description: This is the voice id used when using text to speech.

text-to-speech-language.description: This is the voice language when using TTS.

recorded-datetime.description: This is the timestamp of record time fo the file

index.description: The index of the file, Example "2" for greeting 2. In create (POST) requests index cab be omitted and the API will select a new index automatically.

script.description: This is a simple explanation of what the audio is and can include what is said in the audio file for explanation.

convert.description: Setting yes will allow the API to attempt to convert the file from accepted formats into the correct format for media playback

encoding.description: Used when providing base64 encoded data to give the content tupe of the file.

base64_file.description: This is a base64 encoded string formated without line breaks or spaces.

voice_language.description: This is the language that can be requested when using TTS feature. Leave of to have API lookup configs from the defaults or domain specific configs.

voice_id.description: This value will allow you to select the voice to be used for TTS applications. Value must be valid or will cause errors. leave off request to have the API lookup defaults from system and/or domain.

device-provisioning-line-1-enabled.description: This setting when enable will request caller id blocking on outbound calls requesting privacy settings.  This may lower the ability to connect certain calls if not setup properly to support privacy settings.

device-provisioning-line-2-enabled.description: This setting when enable will request caller id blocking on outbound calls requesting privacy settings.  This may lower the ability to connect certain calls if not setup properly to support privacy settings.

messagesession-remote.description: This is the UserID (ie, 1234@portal) or the phonenumber of the recipient(s) of the messages. If more than one, will be comma seperated

messagesession-muted.description: This is whether or not the message session is muted or not. "yes" or "no"

messagesession-id.description: This is the identifier for the session

messagesession-sms-number.description: This is the SMS number used to send outbound. Will be null if not an SMS message session

messagesession-last-datetime.description: This is the last time the session had an inbound or outbound message

messagesession-start-datetime.description: This is the when the message session started

messagesession-last-message.description: This is the last message in the messagesession

messagesession-last-sender.description: This shows the UID or the phonenumber of who was the last message sender

messagesession-last-status.description: This is the last status of the messagesession. Will contain read/unread status

messagesession-last-email-reminder-datet.description: This is when the last email reminder was sent out notifiying the user of a missed message

messagesession-reciever-hostname.description: This is the hostname of the last received message

messagesession-queue-termination.description: This is which SMS queue the messagesession is assigned to

messagesession-queue-status.description: This is the queue status of the messagesession

messagesession-queue-session-id.description: This is the queue session id placed on the messagesession

messagesession-agent-start-datetime.description: This is when the messagesession was started by the agent

messagesession-last-type.description: This is the last message type of the messagesession

messagesession-last-media-type.description: This is the last media type of the messagesession

messagesession-last-media-size.description: This is the size of the last media of the messagesession

messagesession-type.description: This is the type of messagesession

messagesession-name.description: This is the name of the messagesession. Names can be assigned to group chats

messagesession-participants.description: This is a comma seperated list of participants within a group chat or group sms messagesession

id.description: This is the ID of the message

timestamp.description: This is when the message was recieved or sent

direction.description: This is the direction of the message. Either term for outbound or orig for inbound

from-number.description: This is the SMS number the message came from. Is not set for chat messages

from-user-id.description: This is the User ID the message came from. Not set for offnet numbers

from-video-attendee-id.description: This is the video attendee id the message came from if sent within a video call

from-user-agent.description: This is the HTTP USER AGENT the message came from

dialed.description: This is the number that this message is sent to. This can be comma seperated list of numbers if more than one in a group MMS session. Or null for chat sessions

text.description: This is the text sent in the message

terminating-user-id.description: This is the receiving User ID of the message

terminating-number.description: This is the number that this message is sent to. This can be comma seperated list of numbers if more than one in a group MMS session. Or null for chat sessions

status.description: This is the status of the message

video-instance-id.description: This is the video instance ID of the message if the message was sent from a video session.

media-type.description: This is the media type of the message if applicable

media-size.description: This is the media size of the message if applicable

deleted-datetime.description: This is the deleted timestamp of the message if the message was deleted by a host in a video meeting

anyOf.description: This is when the message was recieved or sent

messagesession-reciever-hostname.description: This is the receiver hostname for the message

messagesession-id.description: This is the messagesession ID for this message

messagesession-participants.description: This is the comma seperated participants of the messagesession associated with this message

message.description: This is the text that will be sent as the message

anyOf.description: A single domain user or phonenumber
An array of domain users or phonenumbers in string format

destination.description: This is a single user or phonenumber for chat and SMS respectively. If sending to multiple recipients, then should be an array of users or phonenumbers

from-number.description: This is used only for SMS. This is the user's number used to send outbound for SMS

data.description: This is used only for media chat or MMS. This is the base64 data of the media file to be sent

mime-type.description: This is used only for media chat or MMS. This is the mime type of the media file to be sent

size.description: This is used only for media chat or MMS. This is the size in bytes of the media file being sent

application.description: This is the application of the number. Can be for a user, callqueue, or available

carrier.description: This is the carrier the SMS number will send and receive messages through

dest.description: This is the destination for the SMS number if assigned. Can be a user or a call queue

mms-capable.description: This is whether or not the SMS number can send MMS messages

group-mms-capable.description: This is whether or not the SMS number can send group messages

voicemail-reminder-phonecall-enabled.description: This setting when enable will request caller id blocking on outbound calls requesting privacy settings.  This may lower the ability to connect certain calls if not setup properly to support privacy settings.

voicemail-reminder-sms-enabled.description: This setting when enable will request caller id blocking on outbound calls requesting privacy settings.  This may lower the ability to connect certain calls if not setup properly to support privacy settings.

voicemail-reminder-email-enabled.description: This setting when enable will request caller id blocking on outbound calls requesting privacy settings.  This may lower the ability to connect certain calls if not setup properly to support privacy settings.

voicemail-reminder-control-server.description: Current server managing the reminders for the user

id.description: This is the main subscription id. This will be needed to edit and delete the subscription.

model.description: This is the type of data that will be sent. you will need to reqest multiple subscriptions if you want more than one model. Addtional details available in guide at https://docs.ns-api.com/docs/event-subscriptions-1

post-url.description: This is the url the API will be posting data to. Should be in the format including https://. Can make a custom port if needed and the URL should have a valid SSL certificate as well. Http:// is supproted but not ideal for production.

subscription-geo-support.description: When enabled  this setting will turn on voicemail for the user.

reseller.description: This is the reseller that the subscription will filter data for. * will be used for all resellers, but only valid with Super User scope on the request.

domain.description: This is the domain that the subscription will filter data for. "*" will be used for all domains, but only valid with Super User scope

user.description: This is the user that the subscription will filter data for. * will be used for all users.

subscription-creation-datetime.description: This is a timestamp of creation of the event subscrtipion.

subscription-expires-datetime.description: This timestamp in the format of YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS will be with the subscription will expire. After it expires it will be removed after some amount of time. If left blank it will set the expires time to the expires time of the timed token used, if using a APIkey it will be 20 years from the creation date that it will set for the expires for the new subscription

preferred-server.description: This is the server hostname for the prefered server for this subscription, meaning if this server is up and online it will be the one sending the events. It wil fail back to this servre after 60s of stability.

current-active-server.description: This is the current server that is running this schedule. It will chagne only when a new server takes over and is a read only field.

status.description: This is the status of the schedule. A new schedule will get "pending" till it gets some data to send and once successfull it will go to "active". If any attempts get a error over a interval it will toggle this field to "error" and reset only once it gets a clean interval of posts.

error-count.description: This is the number of attempted post's that resulted in a error, either an inability to send to a server (network,dns, timeout, etc..) or a response code that is above a 302. This value can be "reset" by setting it to 0 in a update request, but only 0 is allowed value.

posts-count.description: This is the number of attempted post's in this schedule. This includes error and success attempts. This value can be "reset" by setting it to 0 in a update request, but only 0 is allowed value.

call-recording-remote-wav.description: Thie file location on the remote archive path.

call-recording-remote-wav-size-kilobytes.description: The file size in bytes which, if set, suggests that the file is located in remote storage

call-recording-remote-storage-config-id.description: The config id for the file 's remote location.

call-recording-match-pattern.description: This is the case id or the recording match the was used to trigger the request.

call-recording-retrieved-datetime.description: This is the time at which the recoridng was requested.

file-duration-seconds.description: The duration of the recording in seconds.

file-access-url.description: The main url to access the recording.

geo-call-id.description: Corresponding Orig Call ID, if call_id is for a geo tunneled entry

file-size-kilobytes.description: The size of the recording in kilobytes

call-term-user.description: This is the new forward location for the call to be transfered to. Do NOT set when using for attended transfer process.

hostname.description: Required only for supervised/assisted transfers, this is the hostname where the call currently resides.

call-destination-session-id.description: Required only for supervised/assisted transfers, this is the session identifier that is required to help the switch merge in the correct call.

attendant-name.description: This is the main name for the auto attendant. Should be used to explain the main purpose for the auto attendant.

starting-prompt.description: This is a "id" of sorts making this auto attendant unique in terms of this user and dial plan. Will be needed when reading details on a specific AA or making any modifications including removal.

read-path.description: Shown only on a read of multiple auto attendants, will assist in getting the path for reading specific Attendant details.

ordinal-order.description: This is the index of the audio file.

file-size-kilobytes.description: This value will be a formated filesize of the file in kilobytes

file-access-url.description: This will be URL of the file for remote playback.

file-script-text.description: This is the script that was used for text to speech and also used to create a new file.

text-to-speech-voice-id.description: This is the voice id used when using text to speech.

text-to-speech-language.description: This is the voice language when using TTS.

anyOf.description: Enabled will trigger music on hold for the domain and allow it to be customized.

hostname.description: FQDN of the server the files were uploaded from.

domain.description: The domain name of the domain being restored.

type.description: This will specify the kind of backup that was used. This will either be "nightly" or "manual".

index.description: The index number of the backup. This can be found within the defaulted file name on AWS.

confirmation-value.description: First read restore data with the following call: get_restore. his will give you the confirmation value

description.description: The description about the meeting

current_timestamp.description: The current timestamp value of the meeting

meeting-calendar-invite-id.description: The read only value for the meeting to use with calendar integration

meeting-ordinal-sequence.description: The read only value for the meeting with manage the calendar integrations

core-server.description: The core server which the meeting was created on and events are driven from

chat-session-id.description: The read only value identifying the meeting chat room session

meeting-registration-id.description: The token value for guest to use to register for a webinar

meeting-instance-id.description: The instance value of a meeting with used to associate with a recording or differentiating recurring meetings

meeting-name.description: The descriptive name of the meeting

meeting-type.description: The meeting category either the standard meeting 'conference' or a webinar style meeting by using 'presentation'

limits-max-participants.description: The maximum number of participants allows in the meeting

meeting-status.description: The current status of the meeting

meeting-schedule-start-datetime.description: The scheduled start time for the meeting

meeting-schedule-duration-minutes.description: The scheduled length of the meeting

meeting-schedule-recurrence-rule.description: The read only value describing the recuring nature of the meeting using RFC 5545

meeting-video-allowed-from.description: The value indicating which attendee types can share video

meeting-audio-allowed-from.description: The value indicating which attendee types can share audio

meeting-chat-enabled.description: The value indicating if chat is enabled for this meeting

meeting-chat-history-enabled.description: The value indicating if chat is enabled for this meeting

meeting-question-answer-enabled.description: The value indicating if question and answer feature is enabled

meeting-recording-enabled.description: The value indicating if question and answer feature is enabled

meeting-join-require-invite-enabled.description: The value indicating if question and answer feature is enabled

meeting-join-require-passcode-enabled.description: The value indicating if question and answer feature is enabled

meeting-require-host-to-begin-enabled.description: The value indicating if question and answer feature is enabled

meeting-allow-changes-from-hosts-enabled.description: The value indicating if chat is enabled for this meeting

meeting-hide-attendee-list-enabled.description: The value indicating if question and answer feature is enabled

meeting-lock-enabled.description: The value indicating if question and answer feature is enabled

meeting-lock-allowed.description: The value indicating if the meeting is allowed to be locked

meeting-room-enabled.description: The value indicating if question and answer feature is enabled

meeting-dial-in-number-blob.description: The number for audio-only users to dial in to the meeting

meeting-passcode.description: The passcode used to enter meeting if it is required

meeting-previous-presenter-attendee-id.description: The value of the previous attendee that prsented their screen

meeting-presenter-attendee-id.description: The value of the current attendee that is sharing their screen

meeting-video-layout.description: The current meeting layout default for entire meeting

last-modified-datetime.description: The timestamp of the last time the meeting was modified

meeting-instance-actual-start-datetime.description: The timestamp the current meeting instance was started

config-name.description: This is the name of the configuration

config-value.description: This is the configuration value. Can be a string, boolean, or integer

description.description: This describes the configuration

config-name.description: This is the name of the configuration being defined

description.description: This describes the configuration being defined

default.description: This is the default for the configuration. If no scope-specific default is set, this value will be used

code-version.description: This is the code version this definition starts to become applicable

data-type.description: This is the data type allowed for this configuration

config-def-allow-hostname.description: When enabled  this setting will turn on voicemail for the user.

config-def-allow-reseller-access.description: When enabled  this setting will turn on voicemail for the user.

config-def-default-basic-user.description: This is the default for the configuration. If no scope-specific default is set, this value will be used

config-def-default-call-center-superviso.description: This is the default for the configuration. If no scope-specific default is set, this value will be used

config-def-default-office-manager.description: This is the default for the configuration. If no scope-specific default is set, this value will be used

config-def-default-reseller.description: This is the default for the configuration. If no scope-specific default is set, this value will be used

config-def-default-super-user.description: This is the default for the configuration. If no scope-specific default is set, this value will be used

tags.description: Any tags, comma seperated, that should be applied to the defintion

connection-orig-match-pattern.description: This is the connection's origination match pattern

description.description: This is the connection's description

connection-audio-relay-enabled.description: Available options are "yes", "optional", "no". "Yes" will set the connection audio realy to "automatic". "Optional" will set it to "none". "No" will set it to "never". Default will be "yes".

dial-plan.description: This is the connection's dial plan

dial-policy.description: This is the connection's dial policy

limits-max-active-calls-orig.description: This is the connection's limit of maximum originating calls

limits-max-active-calls-term.description: This is the connection's limit of maximum terminating calls

limits-max-active-calls-total.description: This is the connection's limit of maximum total calls

connection-linked-billing-user.description: This is the connection's linked billing user

connection-sip-registration-username.description: This is the connection's authorized username

connection-sip-registration-realm.description: This is the connection's authentication realm

connection-orig-enabled.description: When enabled  this setting will turn on voicemail for the user.

connection-term-enabled.description: When enabled  this setting will turn on voicemail for the user.

connection-translation-request-host.description: This is the connection's translation request host.

connection-translation-destination-host.description: This is the connection's translation destination host. This typically comes from the connection-orig-match-pattern. Defaults to ''

connection-translation-request-user.description: This is the connection's translation request user. This is typically set to [*]. Defaults to ""

connection-translation-destination-user.description: This is the connection's translation destination user. This is typically set to [*]. Defaults to ""

connection-translation-source-user.description: This is the connection's translation source user. This is typically set to [*]. Defaults to ""

connection-source-ip-checking-enabled.description: This setting when enable will request caller id blocking on outbound calls requesting privacy settings.  This may lower the ability to connect certain calls if not setup properly to support privacy settings.

connection-include-server-header-enabled.description: This setting when enable will request caller id blocking on outbound calls requesting privacy settings.  This may lower the ability to connect certain calls if not setup properly to support privacy settings.

connection-relay-comfort-noise-enabled.description: This will set whether there is an RTP Relay comfort noise. Can be set to yes or no. If not set, will be set to system-default

connection-pcmu-only-enabled.description: This setting when enable will request caller id blocking on outbound calls requesting privacy settings.  This may lower the ability to connect certain calls if not setup properly to support privacy settings.

connection-enforce-minimum-duration-enab.description: This setting when enable will request caller id blocking on outbound calls requesting privacy settings.  This may lower the ability to connect certain calls if not setup properly to support privacy settings.

connection-bind-to-alternate-interface-e.description: This setting when enable will request caller id blocking on outbound calls requesting privacy settings.  This may lower the ability to connect certain calls if not setup properly to support privacy settings.

connection-block-video-in-sdp-enabled.description: This setting when enable will request caller id blocking on outbound calls requesting privacy settings.  This may lower the ability to connect certain calls if not setup properly to support privacy settings.

connection-sip-get-new-dialog-destinatio.description: This will set the new dialog destination. Values available are contact-header or via-header. Default would be default-ip

connection-sip-get-response-destination-.description: This will set the response desination. Values available will be contact-header or via-header. Default will be default-ip

connection-require-encrypted-audio-enabl.description: This will set whether audio encryption is required. Options availabe are "optional" and "yes". Defaults to no

connection-sip-transport-protocol.description: This will set the SIP transport protocol. Options are "TCP and "TLS". Default will be UDP

connection-check-orig-matching-sip-heade.description: This will set whether to check whether the origination matches the SIP header. Options are "to", "request", or "contact". Default will default to "from"

connection-sip-session-timer-enabled.description: This will set the SIP session timer. Options are setting to "no" or not setting at all, which will default to the system-default

connection-prevent-rtp-port-change-enabl.description: This setting when enable will request caller id blocking on outbound calls requesting privacy settings.  This may lower the ability to connect certain calls if not setup properly to support privacy settings.

connection-hide-post-dial-delay-with-rin.description: This setting when enable will request caller id blocking on outbound calls requesting privacy settings.  This may lower the ability to connect certain calls if not setup properly to support privacy settings.

connection-remote-ringback-handling.description: This will set the remote ringback handling. Options are "no-ringback" and "local-only". Default will be set to "allowed".

connection-block-media-in-sip-180-ringin.description: This setting when enable will request caller id blocking on outbound calls requesting privacy settings.  This may lower the ability to connect certain calls if not setup properly to support privacy settings.

connection-allow-mid-call-uri-updates-en.description: This setting when enable will request caller id blocking on outbound calls requesting privacy settings.  This may lower the ability to connect certain calls if not setup properly to support privacy settings.

connection-record-all-calls-enabled.description: This setting when enable will request caller id blocking on outbound calls requesting privacy settings.  This may lower the ability to connect certain calls if not setup properly to support privacy settings.

connection-custom-p-asserted-id-format.description: This will set the PAId of the connection. Example input "sample" will translate to "PAId=sample" in call rules

connection-require-sip-authentication-en.description: This setting when enable will request caller id blocking on outbound calls requesting privacy settings.  This may lower the ability to connect certain calls if not setup properly to support privacy settings.

utc-offset.description: This is the utc offset of the connection. Example "-7" for US/Pacific

time-zone.description: This is the timezone of the connection

connection-sip-authenticate-as-client-en.description: This setting when enable will request caller id blocking on outbound calls requesting privacy settings.  This may lower the ability to connect certain calls if not setup properly to support privacy settings.

connection-is-carrier-trunk.description: This setting when enable will request caller id blocking on outbound calls requesting privacy settings.  This may lower the ability to connect certain calls if not setup properly to support privacy settings.

call-routing.description: Currently not supported in V2 API.

aud.description: The "aud" (audience) claim identifies the recipients that the JWT is    intended for.  Each principal intended to process the JWT MUST    identify itself with a value in the audience claim.  If the principal    processing the claim does not identify itself with a value in the    "aud" claim when this claim is present, then the JWT MUST be    rejected.  In the general case, the "aud" value is an array of case-    sensitive strings, each containing a StringOrURI value.  In the    special case when the JWT has one audience, the "aud" value MAY be a    single case-sensitive string containing a StringOrURI value.  The    interpretation of audience values is generally application specific.    Use of this claim is OPTIONAL.

exp.description:
The "exp" (expiration time) claim identifies the expiration time on
   or after which the JWT MUST NOT be accepted for processing.  The
   processing of the "exp" claim requires that the current date/time
   MUST be before the expiration date/time listed in the "exp" claim.
 Implementers MAY provide for some small leeway, usually no more than
   a few minutes, to account for clock skew.  Its value MUST be a number
   containing a NumericDate value.  Use of this claim is OPTIONAL.

iat.description: The "iat" (issued at) claim identifies the time at which the JWT was    issued.  This claim can be used to determine the age of the JWT.  Its    value MUST be a number containing a NumericDate value.  Use of this    claim is OPTIONAL.

iss.description: The "iss" (issuer) claim identifies the principal that issued the    JWT.  The processing of this claim is generally application specific.    The "iss" value is a case-sensitive string containing a StringOrURI    value.  Use of this claim is OPTIONAL.

jti.description: The "jti" (JWT ID) claim provides a unique identifier for the JWT.    The identifier value MUST be assigned in a manner that ensures that    there is a negligible probability that the same value will be    accidentally assigned to a different data object; if the application    uses multiple issuers, collisions MUST be prevented among values    produced by different issuers as well.  The "jti" claim can be used    to prevent the JWT from being replayed.  The "jti" value is a case-    sensitive string.  Use of this claim is OPTIONAL.

sub.description: The "sub" (subject) claim identifies the principal that is the    subject of the JWT.  The claims in a JWT are normally statements    about the subject.  The subject value MUST either be scoped to be    locally unique in the context of the issuer or be globally unique.    The processing of this claim is generally application specific.  The    "sub" value is a case-sensitive string containing a StringOrURI    value.  Use of this claim is OPTIONAL.

time_out.description: The time is the number of miliseconds the JWT will be valid for.

territory.description: This is the reseller that the token will be limited to.

user.description: This is the user that the token will be limited to.

user_email.description: The user's email address if defined.

displayName.description: The user's display name (first + last) if defined.

apiversion.description: The version of the netsapiens API used in token generation

token.description: This is the JWT token that would need to be saved for use in addtional requests.

refresh.description: This is a JWT but only capable of being used to regenerate a new JWT token and not for authenticated requests.

timeframe-specific-dates-begin-date.description: Start date of the specific date period - format: YYYYMMDD

timeframe-specific-dates-begin-time.description: Start time of the specific date period - format: hhmm (24 hour)

timeframe-specific-dates-end-date.description: End date of the specific date period - format: YYYYMMDD

timeframe-specific-dates-end-time.description: End time of the specific date period - format: hhmm (24 hour)

timeframe-recurrence-type.description:
```doesNotRecur```: no recurrence

```monthly-on-last-day```: every month on the last day of the month

```monthly-on-first-last```: monthly on the first or last ordinal weekday, e.g.: the first Friday, the last Thursday - note: this will produce potentially unexpected behavior if used with a date that is not the first or last occurrence of that particular weekday

```monthly-on-ordinal-weekday```: can be used for any ordinal weekday that is NOT the first or last occurrence of that weekday in the given month e.g. the third Friday, the second Monday

```monthly-date```: every month on the same day, e.g.: the 14th day, the 29th day - note: if there are months in which the “day” does not occur, the date will not be active during that month, e.g.: the 31st day of February

```yearly-on-selected-dates```: every year on the exact month and day, e.g.: April 30th, September 4th

```annual-month-day```: functionally identical to ```yearly-on-selected-dates```

```yearly-first```: every year on the first ordinal weekday of the exact month, e.g.: the first Monday of every June, the first Friday of every August - note: this will produce potentially unexpected behavior if used with a date that is not the first occurrence of that particular weekday

```yearly-last```: every year on the last ordinal weekday of the exact month, e.g.: the last Friday of every July, the last Tuesday of every November - note: this will produce potentially unexpected behavior if used with a date that is not the last occurrence of that particular weekday

```custom```: if this option is selected the fields ```timeframe-recurrence-custom-interval```, ```timeframe-recurrence-custom-interval-option```, and ```timeframe-recurrence-custom-interval-count``` must be set correctly to produce any behavior

timeframe-recurrence-custom-interval.description:
Must be combined with the field ```timeframe-recurrence-custom-interval-count```

```days```: recur every ```timeframe-recurrence-custom-interval-count``` days
```weeks```: recur every ```timeframe-recurrence-custom-interval-count``` weeks
```months```: recur every ```timeframe-recurrence-custom-interval-count``` months
```years```: recur every ```timeframe-recurrence-custom-interval-count``` years

timeframe-recurrence-custom-interval-cou.description:
Specifies the recurrence count based on the period specified in ```timeframe-recurrence-custom-interval-count``` e.g. 'every 6 days' or 'every 3 months'

If the field ```timeframe-recurrence-custom-interval-count``` is set to ```months``` this field determines the count in combination with the option set in ```timeframe-recurrence-custom-interval-option```, if ```timeframe-recurrence-custom-interval``` is set to ```years``` the count is fixed to a value of 1 and will recur every year according to the option set in ```timeframe-recurrence-custom-interval-option```

timeframe-recurrence-custom-interval-opt.description:
If ```timeframe-recurrence-custom-interval``` is set to ```days``` or ```weeks``` this field is not used. This field is for ```months``` and ```years``` only.

For ```timeframe-recurrence-custom-interval``` of ```months```:
```monthly-same-day```: recur on the same day of the month, e.g.: the 14th day, the 29th day - note: if there are months in which the “day” does not occur, the date will not be active during that month, e.g.: the 31st day of February
```monthly-ordinal-weekday```:  recur on the same ordinal weekday e.g. the third Friday, the second Monday
```monthly-first-last-weekday```: recur on the first or last instance of the selected weekday in the month, e.g.: the first Wednesday, the last Tuesday - note: this will produce potentially unexpected behavior if used with a date that is not the first or last occurrence of that particular weekday
```monthly-on-last-day```: recur on the last day of the month - note: this will produce potentially unexpected behavior if used with a date that is not the last day of the month

For ```timeframe-recurrence-custom-interval``` of ```years```:
```yearly-same-day```: recur on the same day of the same month, e.g.: the 10th of May, the 20th of October
```yearly-ordinal-weekday```: recur on the same ordinal weekday of the same month, e.g.: the second Tuesday of April, the third Thursday of November
```yearly-first-last-weekday```: recur on the first or last weekday of the same month, e.g.: the third Wednesday of August, the first Tuesday of December - note: this will produce potentially unexpected behavior if used with a date that is not the first or last occurrence of that particular weekday

timeframe-recurrence-end-option.description:
```never```: recurrence will continue indefinitely

```onADate```: recurrence will end on a date, specified in ```timeframe-recurrence-end-date```

timeframe-recurrence-end-date.description: End date of the recurrence - format: YYYYMMDD

timeframe-recurrence-rrule.description: The RRULE string, if recurrence is configured

timeframe-next-observance-datetime.description: List of the upcoming occurrences of the specific date if recurrence is configured, formatted as epoch timestamp in seconds

timeframe-is-v1.description: Will be set to ```true``` if the specific date is part of a timeframe created before v44.1

timeframe-holiday-uuid.description: UUID of the holiday as provided by the Holiday API

timeframe-holiday-name.description: Name of holiday as provided by the Holiday API

timeframe-holiday-begin-date.description: First desired occurrence of the holiday - format: YYYYMMDD

country-abbreviation.description: Country of the holiday as provided by the Holiday API

state-province-abbreviation.description: Region of the holiday as provided by the Holiday API, formatted according to ISO 3166-2 standard.e.g.:```US-CA``` for California, United States```MX-SIN``` for Sinaloa, Mexico

holiday-api-location-hint.description: Locations of the holiday exactly as provided by the Holiday API

timeframe-holiday-begin-time-1.description: Beginning of the first time period during which the time frame will take effect on the holiday - format: hhmm

timeframe-holiday-end-time-1.description: End of the first time period during which the time frame will take effect on the holiday - format: hhmm

timeframe-holiday-begin-time-2.description: Beginning of the second time period, if there is one, during which the time frame will take effect on the holiday - format: hhmm

timeframe-holiday-end-time-2.description: End of the second time period, if there is one, during which the time frame will take effect on the holiday - format: hhmm

timeframe-workweek-list.description:
Comma-separated list containing days of the week to be considered workdays (minimum of 4, maximum of 6).
e.g.: ```M,T,Th,F,S,Su``` would produce a workweek of Monday, Tuesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, and Sunday

timeframe-recurrence-type.description:
```never```: No recurrence.
```yearlyOnHoliday```: Recurs every year on the holiday.

timeframe-recurrence-end-option.description:
```never```: recurs indefinitely.

```onDate```: ends on the date specified in ```recurrence_end_date``` - note: both fields must be set to produce the desired behavior

timeframe-expiry-datetime.description: End date of recurrence - format: YYYYMMDD

timeframe-weekly-sunday-begin-time-1.description: Beginning of the first time period during which the time frame will take effect on Sundays - format: hhmm

timeframe-weekly-sunday-end-time-1.description: End of the first time period during which the time frame will take effect on Sundays - format: hhmm

timeframe-weekly-sunday-begin-time-2.description: Beginning of the second time period, if there is one, during which the time frame will take effect on Sundays - format: hhmm

timeframe-weekly-sunday-end-time-2.description: End of the second time period, if there is one, during which the time frame will take effect on Sundays - format: hhmm

timeframe-weekly-monday-begin-time-1.description: Beginning of the first time period during which the time frame will take effect on Mondays - format: hhmm

timeframe-weekly-monday-end-time-1.description: End of the first time period during which the time frame will take effect on Mondays - format: hhmm

timeframe-weekly-monday-begin-time-2.description: Beginning of the second time period, if there is one, during which the time frame will take effect on Mondays - format: hhmm

timeframe-weekly-monday-end-time-2.description: End of the second time period, if there is one, during which the time frame will take effect on Mondays - format: hhmm

timeframe-weekly-tuesday-begin-time-1.description: Beginning of the first time period during which the time frame will take effect on Tuesdays - format: hhmm

timeframe-weekly-tuesday-end-time-1.description: End of the first time period during which the time frame will take effect on Tuesdays - format: hhmm

timeframe-weekly-tuesday-begin-time-2.description: Beginning of the second time period, if there is one, during which the time frame will take effect on Tuesdays - format: hhmm

timeframe-weekly-tuesday-end-time-2.description: End of the second time period, if there is one, during which the time frame will take effect on Tuesdays - format: hhmm

timeframe-weekly-wednesday-begin-time-1.description: Beginning of the first time period during which the time frame will take effect on Wednesdays - format: hhmm

timeframe-weekly-wednesday-end-time-1.description: End of the first time period during which the time frame will take effect on Wednesdays - format: hhmm

timeframe-weekly-wednesday-begin-time-2.description: Beginning of the second time period, if there is one, during which the time frame will take effect on Wednesdays - format: hhmm

timeframe-weekly-wednesday-end-time-2.description: End of the second time period, if there is one, during which the time frame will take effect on Wednesdays - format: hhmm

timeframe-weekly-thursday-begin-time-1.description: Beginning of the first time period during which the time frame will take effect on Thursdays - format: hhmm

timeframe-weekly-thursday-end-time-1.description: End of the first time period during which the time frame will take effect on Thursdays - format: hhmm

timeframe-weekly-thursday-begin-time-2.description: Beginning of the second time period, if there is one, during which the time frame will take effect on Thursdays - format: hhmm

timeframe-weekly-thursday-end-time-2.description: End of the second time period, if there is one, during which the time frame will take effect on Thursdays - format: hhmm

timeframe-weekly-friday-begin-time-1.description: Beginning of the first time period during which the time frame will take effect on Fridays - format: hhmm

timeframe-weekly-friday-end-time-1.description: End of the first time period during which the time frame will take effect on Fridays - format: hhmm

timeframe-weekly-friday-begin-time-2.description: Beginning of the second time period, if there is one, during which the time frame will take effect on Fridays - format: hhmm

timeframe-weekly-friday-end-time-2.description: End of the second time period, if there is one, during which the time frame will take effect on Fridays - format: hhmm

timeframe-weekly-saturday-begin-time-1.description: Beginning of the first time period during which the time frame will take effect on Saturdays - format: hhmm

timeframe-weekly-saturday-end-time-1.description: End of the first time period during which the time frame will take effect on Saturdays - format: hhmm
