timeframe-weekly-saturday-end-time-2.description: End of the second time period, if there is one, during which the time frame will take effect on Saturdays - format: hhmm

timeframe-recurrence-type.description:
```weekly```: recur every week
```custom```: if this option is selected the fields ```timeframe-recurrence-begin-date```, ```timeframe-recurrence-custom-interval```, ```timeframe-recurrence-custom-interval-option```, and ```timeframe-recurrence-custom-interval-count``` must be set correctly to produce any behavior

timeframe-recurrence-begin-date.description: Start date of the recurrence - format: YYYYMMDD - note: this field is only applicable for the ```timeframe-recurrence-type``` option ```custom```

timeframe-recurrence-custom-interval.description:
Must be combined with the field ```timeframe-recurrence-custom-interval-count```

```weeks```: recur every ```timeframe-recurrence-custom-interval-count``` weeks
```months```: recur every ```timeframe-recurrence-custom-interval-count``` months

timeframe-recurrence-custom-interval-cou.description:
Specifies the recurrence count based on the period specified in ```timeframe-recurrence-custom-interval``` e.g. 'every 6 weeks' or 'every 3 months'

If the field ```timeframe-recurrence-custom-interval``` is set to ```months``` this field determines the count in combination with the option set in ```timeframe-recurrence-custom-interval-option```

timeframe-recurrence-custom-interval-opt.description:
If ```timeframe-recurrence-custom-interval``` is set to ```weeks``` this field is not used. This field is for ```months``` only.
The following options configure recurrence for the Days of Week period according to the start date defined in ```timeframe-recurrence-begin-date```.

For ```timeframe-recurrence-custom-interval``` of ```months```:
```monthly-same-day```: recur on the same day of the month, e.g.: the 14th day, the 29th day - note: if there are months in which the “day” does not occur, the Days fo Week will not be active during that month, e.g.: the 31st day of February
```monthly-ordinal-weekday```:  recur on any ordinal weekday in the given month e.g. the third Friday, the second Monday
```monthly-first-last-weekday```: recur on the first or last instance of the selected weekday in the month, e.g.: the first Wednesday, the last Tuesday - note: this will produce potentially unexpected behavior if used with a date that is not the first or last occurrence of that particular weekday

timeframe-recurrence-end-option.description:
```never```: recurrence will continue indefinitely
```onADate```: recurrence will end on a date, specified in ```timeframe-recurrence-end-date```

timeframe-recurrence-end-date.description: End date of the recurrence - format: YYYYMMDD

timeframe-next-observance-datetime.description: List of the upcoming occurrences of the days of week if ```recurrence``` is configured as ```custom```, formatted as epoch timestamp in seconds

email.description: The email address of the Contact.

time_zone.description: optional, IANA time zone name. Values are those (e.g. US/Central, Europe/London, Asia/Tokyo, etc.) in the IANA Time Zone database (https://www.iana.org/time-zones)

duration.description: Duration of the recording in seconds

size.description: Size of converted file in KByte

size_3.description: The file size in bytes which, if set, suggests that the file is located in remote storage

geo_id.description: Corresponding Orig Call ID, if call_id is for a geo tunneled entry

enabled.description: When enabled  this setting will turn on voicemail for the user.

is-active.description: This is read only option that will be true if this timeframe is active for this user when taking into account current settings, date/time and all timeframes.

phone-numbers-to-reject.description: This is a list of numbers that would be blocked and rejected before any forward or sim ring features are attempted.

enabled.description: This setting when enable will request caller id blocking on outbound calls requesting privacy settings.  This may lower the ability to connect certain calls if not setup properly to support privacy settings.

remove.description: This setting when enable will request caller id blocking on outbound calls requesting privacy settings.  This may lower the ability to connect certain calls if not setup properly to support privacy settings.

parameters.description: This field will provide the values needed to configure the parent feature. These values could be multiple strings or numbers.

call-intelligence-job-id.description: This is the main ID identifying the transcription job and will link all intelligence features.

call-intelligence-processing-status.description: This is the status of the transcription and intelegence work. The data will be enter as soon as the request is made to remote vendor, but might not be availabel fully untill status is "finished"

created-datetime.description: The date-time of the request for transcription.

call-id.description: The linked call-id for the SIP call being recorded. Could be an orig_callid or term_callid depending on who requested recording and type of call.

call-intelligence-features-enabled.description: List of features enabled for request.

call-intelligence-summary.description: The main summary of the call when available.

call-intelligence-topics-top.description: The list of top topics detected on the call based on frequency.

call-intelligence-duration-seconds.description: The duration in seconds for the length of time where detected voice was seen.

call-intelligence-duration-seconds-negat.description: The duration in seconds for the length of time where detected voice was seen with a Negative sentiment

call-intelligence-duration-seconds-neutr.description: The duration in seconds for the length of time where detected voice was seen.

call-intelligence-duration-seconds-posit.description: The duration in seconds for the length of time where detected voice was seen with a Positve sentiment

call-intelligence-topics-all.description: The complete list of detected topics in the conversation

errors.description: If errors are present it will display here.

last-modified-datetime.description: The modified time of the transcription job. Difference between this and created-datetime will be the duration of the job in most cases.

route-id.description: This is an id that will be needed to edit, delete the route as well as manage its route connection rules.

route-destination-match-pattern.description: This is the main route match for the destination.

route-source-match-pattern.description: This is the source route, in most cases this should default to * and not be set otherwise.

route-class.description: This for jurisdictional routing of class. Leave as * for most cases.

route-match-server-hostname.description: This field will limit the route to only be effective on a matching hostname. This allow for localized routing to a closest carrier's POP or other special cases.

route-is-enabled.description: When enabled  this setting will turn on voicemail for the user.

description.description: This is the route description, use to to give a plain english explanation as to what the route is intended to do.

route-method.description: This is the type of route to select the distribution method for the next hop/connection setting.

x-apidog-enum.name: fixed
x-apidog-enum.description: This is the most common case, will use a set order to route to the connections.

x-apidog-enum.name: cost
x-apidog-enum.description: This will use cost in order of smallest to highest to route the call.

x-apidog-enum.name: percentage
x-apidog-enum.description: This will enable a percentage/load balanced approach.

device-models-brand.description: This is the brand of the phone/device

device-models-model.description: This is the model of the phone/device

device-models-type.description: The type of equipemnt, most will be "Device"

device-models-config-format.description: The NDP format that will be used to format the config, this must match a valid format known to the ndp either a preconfigure or custom one, but cannot be just made up.

device-models-button-types.description: Read only concatination of brand and mod

status-login-duration-minutes.description: This is the total time the call center agent's status was set to "online". Starting in v43.2, an agent has to manually change their status from offline to online when they begin their shift; logging into the portal no longer affects the LI stat.

status-available-duration-minutes.description: This is the total time the call center agent was available for calls. This includes time logged in / online. It excludes statuses of unavailable such as break, lunch, meeting, web, and other.

status-unavailable-duration-minutes.description: This is the total time the call center agent was not available for calls. Includes time agent has the offline statuses, such as offline, break, lunch, meeting, web, and other. Excludes online / available.

status-lunch-duration-minutes.description: This is the total time the call center agent's status was set to "lunch".

status-break-duration-minutes.description: This is the total time the call center agent's status was set to "break".

status-meeting-duration-minutes.description: This is the total time the call center agent's status was set to "meeting".

status-other-duration-minutes.description: This is the total time the call center agent's status is not set to available, break, lunch, meeting, or web. Excludes unavailable status.

status-web-duration-minutes.description: This is the total time the call center agent's status was set to "web".

status-custom1-duration-minutes.description: This is the total time the call center agent's status was set to "CUST1".

status-custom2-duration-minutes.description: This is the total time the call center agent's status was set to "CUST2".

status-custom3-duration-minutes.description: This is the total time the call center agent's status was set to "CUST3".

status-custom4-duration-minutes.description: This is the total time the call center agent's status was set to "CUST4".

status-custom5-duration-minutes.description: This is the total time the call center agent's status was set to "CUST5".

status-custom6-duration-minutes.description: This is the total time the call center agent's status was set to "CUST6".

status-custom7-duration-minutes.description: This is the total time the call center agent's status was set to "CUST7".

status-custom8-duration-minutes.description: This is the total time the call center agent's status was set to "CUST8".

calls-total-count.description: Number of calls originating through a Call Queue. Includes answered calls, abandoned calls, forwards, and voicemail.

calls-answered-count.description: Number of calls answered by agent originating through a Call Queue. The call must also terminate with the agent to be counted as a call handled.

calls-presented-count.description: Number of calls that reached the queue to be dispatched to agents. Includes abandoned calls. Excludes forwards and voicemail.

calls-presented-adjusted-count.description: Adjusted number of calls that reached the queue. Excludes calls abandoned in less than 10 seconds.

calls-voicemailed-count.description: Number of calls handled by the automated voicemail system.

calls-forwarded-count.description: Number of calls forwarded to another queue or off-net phone number for handling. Includes forwarded calls to voicemail.

calls-talking-avg-duration-minutes.description: Average number of minutes spent by agent talking per call, on calls originating through a Call Queue. Excludes hold time.

calls-assisted-count.description: Number of calls answered and then transferred to another agent.

calls-after-call-work-avg-duration-secon.description:
Average time an agent spends between the end of a call and submitting call disposition.

This metric is only applied to agents using Single Call Mode.

calls-callback-requests-count.description: Number of calls that requested a callback rather than waiting in the queue.

calls-holding-avg-duration-seconds.description: Average time a caller spends on hold with an agent. Excludes waiting time in the Call Queue.

calls-service-level-percent.description: The ratio of calls meeting the configurable service level agreement.

calls-presented-percent.description: Percentage of calls that landed in the queue and were offered to an agent.

calls-abandoned-count.description: Number of calls that abandoned the queue before being answered by an agent.

calls-abandoned-adjusted-count.description: Adjusted number of calls that abandoned the queue. Excludes calls abandoned in less than 10 seconds.

calls-abandoned-rate.description: Percentage of calls offered that were abandoned before being offered to an agent.

calls-abandoned-adjusted-rate.description: Percentage of calls offered that were abandoned in under 10 seconds.

calls-handling-avg-duration-minutes.description: Average time an agent spent on a call. Includes Talk Time (TT), Hold Time (AH), and Disposition Time (ACW).

calls-queueing-avg-duration-seconds.description: Average number of seconds a caller spent in the selected queue before being dispatched to an agent. If none selected, total for all queues will be displayed.

outbound-calls-attempted-count.description: Number of outbound call attempts by an agent. Excludes on-net calls and conference calls.

outbound-calls-answered-count.description: Number of outbound calls by agent answered by a remote party. Includes calls answered by voicemail. Excludes on-net calls and conference calls.

outbound-calls-duration-minutes.description: Number of minutes spent by an agent on outbound calls. Includes talk and hold time. Excludes call center calls, on-net calls, and conference calls.

outbound-calls-avg-duration-minutes.description: Average length of time spent by an agent on outbound calls. Excludes call center calls, on-net calls, and conference calls.

inbound-calls-presented-count.description: Number of attempted calls inbound to agent. Includes call center calls. Excludes on-net calls and conference calls.

inbound-calls-answered-count.description: Number of inbound answered calls to an agent. Includes call center calls. Excludes on-net calls and conference calls.

inbound-calls-duration-minutes.description: Number of minutes spent by an agent on inbound calls. Includes call center calls. Includes talk and hold time. Excludes on-net calls and conference calls.

inbound-calls-avg-duration-minutes.description: Average length of time spent by an agent on inbound calls. Includes call center calls. Excludes on-net calls and conference calls.

sms-conversations-count.description: Number of SMS sessions handled through a Call Queue.

sms-handling-avg-duration-seconds.description: Average time an agent spent handling an SMS session from the queue, starting from the moment they accept the message until it is terminated.

calls-talking-duration-minutes.description: The number of minutes spent by an agent on answered calls originating through a Call Queue.

calls-unanswered-count.description: Number of calls originating through a call queue offered to an agent but not answered. This includes multiple attempts if a call loops through all agents, but excludes unanswered simultaneous ring calls.

phoneconfiguration-name.description: This is the main name for the phone configuration when used as a template

phoneconfiguration-mac-address.description: When phone configuration is applied to a specific phone (not template) this will be a valid mac

phoneconfiguration-description.description: This is a plain description of the configuration

phoneconfiguration-brand.description: The brand for which this phone configuration will be build for.

phoneconfiguration-model.description: The model specific to brand  for which this phone configuration will be build for.

phoneconfiguration-domain.description: The domain owning this phone configuration

phoneconfiguration-brand.description: This is the brand for sidecar specifically. It likely will match the parent phoneconfiguration-brand setting.

phoneconfiguration-model.description: This is the model specifically for the sidecar unit, it will be different to the model listed at the root level.

phoneconfiguration-sidecars.description: List of "sidecars" for the phone if in use. Use multiple if multiple side car's in use.

dynamic.description: dynamic can be set to true if this is being used in dynamic BLF settings.

phoneconfiguration-extra.description: This is an "extra" object that will be used for easy expansion when needed later. Available optional declerations will be defined below.

group.description: This is the name of the Directory data set. It could be the like of "All", "Coworkers" or a specific entry like the name of a Department.

group-type.description: This is the type of contacts to use, "Standard" or "department"

sort.description: This is the label to use to sort the directory

order.description: This is the sort direction for the directory

id.description: The uid of the user being excluded from the directory.

type.description: The type of contact, most will be "onnet"

exclusions.description: List of excluded entries for the domain

last-modified-datetime.description: The last updated timestamp for the config

domain.description: This is the main name of the domain. This name should be capable of being in SIP enpoints so no special characters or spaces.

call-orig-call-id.description: Unique identifier for the Origination call leg

agent-term-uri.description: SIP URI of the terminating Agent.

agent-term-user.description: This is the extension of the Agent.

agent-term-domain.description: This is the domain of the Agent.

agent-name-first-name.description: This is the first name for the Agent.

agent-name-last-name.description: This is the last name for the Agent.

callqueue.description: Extension id for a preconfigured callqueue

call-dialed-number.description: Dialed Number Identification Service - the number that was called.

agent-talking-duration-seconds.description: Time Agent spent on the call talking to caller.

agent-call-duration-seconds.description: Total time Agent spent on the call including hold time.

queue-duration-seconds.description: Time call spent in the queue before answered by an Agent.

agent-holding-duration-seconds.description: Time Agent spent on hold during the call.

disposition-duration-seconds.description: Disposition / Wrap up time for agent before going Online for next call.

agent-call-start-datetime.description: Time when call was presented to the Agent.

queue-call-start-datetime.description: Time when call entered to the queue.

queue-call-disconnect-datetime.description: Time when call was released from the queue.

agent-call-disconnect-datetime.description: Time when call was released by the Agent.

agent-call-answer-datetime.description: Time when call was answered by the Agent.

call-orig-from-uri.description: Full SIP URI of the caller.

domain.description: The domain this OIDC provider is configured for. Used to scope provider settings to specific domains in multi-tenant environments

provider-id.description: Unique identifier for the provider (e.g., 'google', 'microsoft', 'auth0'). Used in URLs and as a database key

provider-name.description: Human-readable display name shown to users on login buttons and admin interfaces

client-id.description: OAuth 2.0 client identifier issued by the OIDC provider during application registration

client-secret.description: OAuth 2.0 client secret for authenticating to the provider's token endpoint. Should be stored encrypted

discovery-url.description: OpenID Connect discovery document URL (/.well-known/openid-configuration). Used to automatically fetch provider endpoints and configuration

authorization-endpoint.description: OAuth 2.0 authorization endpoint URL where users are redirected to authenticate

token-endpoint.description: OAuth 2.0 token endpoint URL where authorization codes are exchanged for access tokens

userinfo-endpoint.description: OpenID Connect UserInfo endpoint URL for retrieving authenticated user profile information

jwks-uri.description: JSON Web Key Set URI containing public keys for verifying ID token signatures

issuer.description: OIDC issuer identifier. Must exactly match the 'iss' claim in ID tokens for validation

scope.description: Space-separated list of OAuth scopes to request. Should always include 'openid' for OIDC compliance

logo-url.description: Path or URL to provider logo image displayed on login buttons

enabled.description: Whether this provider is active and available for user authentication

display-order.description: Sort order for displaying providers in login UI. Lower numbers appear first

DomainName.description: This is the main organization name. This is used to link resource to its group/tenant/organization/enterprise. ~ and * can be used alone in special cases to mean My Domain (~) and All Domains (\*).

DomainNamePath.description: This is the main organization name. This is used to link resource to its group/tenant/organization/enterprise. Then using in path for GET you can set to "~" to use the domain name linked to your token/key instead of the domain name itself when reading for your own domain.

UserName.description: This is the main user extension for the user account.

UserNamePath.description: This is the main user extension for the user account. ~ can be used to mean "my account" which will extract the user from the token/key used for authentication.

code.description: This is the HTTP error code that was returned.

message.description: This is a more detailed description of what the error was and any potential correction steps.

TimeZone.description: This is the time zone that the user is primarily located in

Scopes.description: Will link this resource with a User Scope granting it certain permisionf to the system for use in API and Portal.

enumDescriptions.call_origid: this is a long descriptions,. sdfdsi sdfdsnes dslkdsflk sdf dsfslkj ererew

enumDescriptions.message: this is a long descriptions,. sdfdsi sdfdsnes dslkdsflk sdf dsfslkj ererew

Synchronous.description: When synchronous is requested with "yes" the response will be a 200 on success and will contain a valid JSON representiation of the new resource. If no or left off request a 202 "Accepted" will be given in a success case and this can be considered a success. Synchronous responses will be a little slower as the API will process the geo replication request and wait till the local copy has been written and can be read back berfore sending a response.

DialruleApplication.description: This is the application that the call will be routed using. It works in partnership with dial-rule-translation-destination-user and dial-rule-translation-destination-host to forward to a next destination.

CallqueuePath.description: When used in path the callqueue value will link to a extension id for a preconfigured callqueue. If using agent actions like "login" you can use "all" to change action for all of the queues the agent is in currently, it can not be used for agent update requests.

AgentPath.description: This is the agent identified to be used to manage a agent in a call queue. The format can be user@domain or sip:device@domain depending on how the queue is indented to be administered.  If you are using a auth method with a valid user level access you can use "~" and the api will assume the agent is user@domain format and grab that from the token.

Timestamp.description: This is the time stamp that the formated via RFC 3339

DeleteType.description: the type of delete to perform

enumDescriptions.meeting: delete a meeting but perserves the DB record

RecordingConfiguration.description: Option whether to record calls or not. Available options are "yes", "yes-with-transcription", "yes-with-transcription-and-sentiment", or "no"

x-apidog-enum.description: Sets recording on with transcription and sentiment

DatetimeStart.description:
This is the start of the time window for a given query. The format is based on RFC3339 but also will support additonal params for use in providing a timezone which will allow the API to give the correct range for a given set of data and will be formated in respone using the same format. 

Supported formats include. 
* 2023-11-27 13:00:00
* 2023-11-27T13:00:00Z
* 2023-11-27T13:00:00-08:00
* 2023-11-27T13:00:00-08:00[US/Pacific]
* 2023-11-27T13:00:00Z[America/Phoenix]

DatetimeEnd.description: This is the end of the time window for a given query. The format is based on RFC3339 but also will support additonal params for use in providing a timezone which will allow the API to give the correct range for a given set of data and will be formated in respone using the same format. Allowed values same as datetime-start.

RecurringTimeframeType.description:
```days-of-week```: Days of Week
```specific-dates```: Specific Dates
```holiday```: Holidays
```custom```: Custom
```always```: Always

RecurringTimeframeHolidayObservance.description:
```observe-on-holiday-only```: Observe only on the exact holiday
```observe-on-holiday-and-next-day```: Observe on the exact holiday and the next day
```observe-on-holiday-and-prior-day```: Observe on the exact holiday and the prior day
```observe-on-exact-date-and-next-workday```: Observe on the exact holiday and the next workday
```observe-on-holiday-and-prior-workday```: Observe on the exact holiday and the prior workday
```observe-on-next-workday```: Observe only on the next workday
```observe-on-prior-workday```: Observe only on the prior workday

Limit.description: This is the limit of the number of objects that will be returned in the read/list API call. This can be used with pagination to help retrieve smaller chunks of data. Should be used in conjunction with Start.

Start.description: This is the start value when using pagination. 0 is the default and the minimum value. For example the default of 0 paired with the default limit of 100 would return the first 100 objects in the data request. A start of 100 and a default limit of 100 would give you the second 100 results in the response.

domain.description: The domain the owns the Dial Policy

permission-match.description: The specific match to check against

permission-mode.description: To allow or deny specific match

permission-reason.description: The description/reason for making the rule.

created-datetime.description: The time the rule was created

permission-id.description: This is the ID fro the permission rule. Its the base64 encode of permission-match, but is needed for edit and delete.

domain.description: The domain that owns the DialPolicy

description.description: The description of the Policy Table

common-name.description: This is the main hostname for the certficate. This must be a FQDN and much post via CNAME or A record to a ip(s) of the cluster

subject-alternative-name.description: These are alterntive names that can be included in the certificate, each one comma seperated must be valid and pointed at the cluster

reseller.description: This is the reseller that owns and has access to the certificate

account-email-address.description: This is the main email address associated with the certificate request, it will be used to auto create an account with LetsEncrypt

status.description: This is the status of the certificate request process. Should be "ok" if not in process of upgrading or renewing

generate-datetime.description: The datetime the certificate was generated

expiration-datetime.description: The datetime the certificate will expire

challenge-type.description: http-01 or dns-01, for most all requests it will be http-01 and you can leave off requests

default-ssl-cert.description: This setting when enable will request caller id blocking on outbound calls requesting privacy settings.  This may lower the ability to connect certain calls if not setup properly to support privacy settings.

replicate-ssl-cert.description: For most cases this will be "yes" or "ndp" with the later being used to create ndp/endpoint specific certifcates replicated to those servers only.

profile-button-index.description: This is the index of the button, starting at 1 and going up.

profile-button-type.description: This is the type of button, they will vary by vendo and each one will link to functions available to the button. linekey is an example.

profile-button-position.description: This are x/y cordinates for the button in conjuction with the image provided. String in the format like  "[1561,830.5]"

profile-button-settings.description: Addtional settings field for the button.

device-models-type.description: The type for the profile, in most cases "phone" is set.

profile-page-count.description: The number of pages (if any) available in the profile

profile-sidecar-support.description: A list (if any) of available sidecar models compatable with the phone

buttons.description: List of Available button and their info

server.description: Optional Server is aggregated by server

ts.description: The timestamp for the value. Modulo the expected interval/step

query.description: The is the query that is sent to insight

step.description: The interval for which insight will split the data up.

phoneconfiguration-button-order.description: This is the button order, It will increment from the first button and will continue through all the sidecar buttons.

phoneconfiguration-button-type.description: Type of button that will link to the NDP server's building of the button. Needs to match oiptions available in vendor/model.

phoneconfiguration-button-feature.description: This is the main feature type. Common ones inlcude line, blf, park,speed_dial, queue_status, timeframe_status

phoneconfiguration-button-target.description: This is the main setting mapping the resouce to the button. example would be the extension or queue name in many cases.

phoneconfiguration-button-label.description: Any lable override will go here if wish to change/force the text to be displayed.

phoneconfiguration-button-locked.description: If wanting to lock a button from editing by UI, set to 1.

targetUser.description: targetUser in cases where editing another user.

dynamic.description: Set to true for dynamic BLF

id.description: The ID for the SMS block . Will be auto generated on addition

requesting-number.description: This is the number that is requesting the block be added against the blocked-number.

blocked-number.description: This is the number that will be blocked from sending new SMS to the requesting-number

domain.description: Domain owning this block
